config.section.0.title: AI SharePoint
config.aiSharePoint.copilot.preferredMod: Model family this extension uses by default (set via **AI SharePoint: List Copilot Models**). Empty = cheapest entitled model (economy-first policy).
config.aiSharePoint.auth.tenantAuthority: Microsoft Entra ID authority for sign-in (MSAL public client). Machine-scoped: workspaces cannot override it. The host must be a known Microsoft login endpoint or listed in `#aiSharePoint.auth.additionalAuthorityHosts#`.
config.aiSharePoint.auth.clientId.markdo: Optional custom Entra application (client) ID for tenants that block the default Microsoft Graph PowerShell first-party app. Requires a public-client app registration with `http://localhost` redirect and delegated `Sites.Read.All` — see the Admin Guide.
config.aiSharePoint.auth.additionalAutho: Extra allowed sign-in authority hosts (beyond the built-in Microsoft login endpoints). Machine-scoped.
config.aiSharePoint.diagnostics.usageCap: Local feature-usage counters for the diagnostics bundle. **Nothing is ever transmitted by the extension** — data leaves the machine only via your explicit export, or the opt-in external telemetry you configure under **Support & Diagnostics → Usage Telemetry** (command: *AI SharePoint: Manage Usage Telemetry*). External telemetry also honors this setting / your VS Code telemetry stance.
Capture local usage counters only while VS Code telemetry is enabled (defers to your org's telemetry stance).
config.aiSharePoint.diagnostics.errorCap: Store redacted error reports locally for the diagnostics bundle. Disable to keep no error history at all.
config.aiSharePoint.lessons.capture.mark: **Opt-in, default off.** Let @sharepoint record *anonymized, generalized* interaction lessons when it self-corrects (e.g. "when the user says 'my Confluence space', scope to their personal space, not global") into a local ledger you can review and export. The lessons are scrubbed of tenant/user identifiers and **nothing is ever transmitted by the extension** — they leave the machine only when you explicitly export the file to share with the plugin developer ("AI SharePoint: Export Lessons Learned").
config.aiSharePoint.sync.allowedRemoteHo: Git hosts that site repositories may push to (ADR-0019). Add your GitHub Enterprise Server host, e.g. `github.corp.example`. Machine-scoped: workspaces cannot override it.
config.aiSharePoint.sync.writePermission: Graph permission the write-back (*Apply to SharePoint*) requests (ADR-0037). **`selected`** asks for `Sites.Selected` — far more likely to be approved, but an admin must grant the app access to each target site (`Grant-PnPAzureADAppSitePermission`, or `POST /sites/{id}/permissions`; see the Admin Guide §4). **`all`** asks for the tenant-wide `Sites.ReadWrite.All` + `Sites.Manage.All`.
Sites.Selected (recommended, least privilege): an admin grants the app access to ONLY the specific site(s). Requires a one-time per-site grant — see the Admin Guide.
Sites.ReadWrite.All + Sites.Manage.All: tenant-wide delegated write across every site you can access. Simpler, but usually needs broad admin consent.
config.aiSharePoint.context.cacheTtlMinu: How long reference-source results are cached locally (ADR-0011). 0 disables caching.
config.aiSharePoint.context.maxResults.m: Default result window — the maximum rows/results per reference-source query (ADR-0012 read-safety cap). Tunable mid-conversation by asking @sharepoint to change the result window; the 200 ceiling always applies.
config.aiSharePoint.context.queryTimeout: Base timeout for a single database query/probe (seconds). Large join probes are automatically granted a longer, cost-estimated budget (up to ~2 minutes, or this value if higher) based on table row counts, so big joins finish instead of timing out at the default.
config.aiSharePoint.context.maxInputToke: Override the model's advertised input-context size (tokens) when Copilot clamps the effective usable context below the native max (#3). `0` = auto-detect: use the model's advertised limit and learn the real ceiling over time from successes and overflow failures. Set a value only if you know your environment's hard clamp.
config.aiSharePoint.proxy.mode.markdownD: How @sharepoint handles words a corporate proxy may block (#4). `warn` flags them; `defang` auto-adjusts outgoing messages to slip past; `off` disables. Repeated network-level failures also trigger proxy-block guidance regardless of this setting.
config.aiSharePoint.proxy.mode.enumDescr: Flag avoid-list words in your message before sending (you rephrase).
Automatically insert an invisible separator into avoid-list words so a content-blocking proxy's match fails, while the model still reads them.
config.aiSharePoint.proxy.blockedTerms.m: Words/phrases a corporate proxy or DLP appliance tends to block (often false positives on ordinary vocabulary). @sharepoint avoids them per `aiSharePoint.proxy.mode`. You can also grow this list at runtime by asking @sharepoint to add a word, or via "AI SharePoint: Manage Proxy Avoid-List".
config.aiSharePoint.context.catalogTtlHo: How long a pre-cached source catalog (Confluence spaces / Jira projects+queues) stays fresh. After expiry the next browse offers a refresh; the expired copy remains usable.
config.aiSharePoint.context.catalogCheck: While pre-caching a large catalog, ask every N seconds whether to continue. Loading pauses while the prompt waits, so the source system is never overtaxed.
config.aiSharePoint.context.allowSchemaI: Allow building **semantic schema indexes** of database sources with Copilot (ADR-0024). Only table/column **names and types** are ever sent — never row data — and always behind an explicit per-source consent. Set `false` to disable organization-wide.
config.aiSharePoint.ldap.tlsRejectUnauth: Validate LDAPS/StartTLS server certificates for LDAP/Active Directory sources (ADR-0020). Keep **true** in production. If LDAPS uses an internal CA not in Node's bundle, prefer installing the CA; only disable for isolated lab testing.
config.aiSharePoint.ldap.useStartTls.mar: For `ldap://` (port 389) sources, upgrade the connection with StartTLS before binding. Ignored for `ldaps://` (implicit TLS).
config.aiSharePoint.ldap.caCertificatesF: Path to a PEM CA bundle trusted for LDAPS/StartTLS connections (e.g. your corporate root + intermediates), **appended** to Node's defaults and the OS trust store. Machine-scoped. Use when the OS-store API is unavailable on your VS Code runtime — see Admin Guide §7.
config.aiSharePoint.ldap.allowRawFilters: Allow a search query that is itself a parenthesized LDAP filter (e.g. `(&(objectClass=user)(department=Sales))`) to be passed through verbatim. **Off by default**: every query is otherwise escaped as an Ambiguous Name Resolution match, so free text — including a chat-driven search — can never inject filter syntax. Enable only for trusted, expert-driven directory queries. Machine-scoped.
Log the **full request/response detail of every integration** — Microsoft Graph (SharePoint, Teams, Outlook), Confluence/Jira, LDAP/Active Directory, SQL Server/PostgreSQL/MySQL/MongoDB, Vertex AI Search, Power BI, MSAL sign-in traffic, and Copilot prompts/responses — to the *AI SharePoint* output channel. **Secrets are redacted in layers**: authorization headers are reduced to their scheme, token-endpoint bodies are withheld entirely, credential-shaped fields are masked, and every line passes the global redaction filter. Database/LDAP **result data is summarized (counts/columns), not dumped**. Local only; never included in diagnostics exports. Expect large output — enable only while debugging.
config.aiSharePoint.ldap.dnsServers.mark: **Machine-scoped.** Internal DNS server IPs used for Active Directory **SRV lookups** (e.g. `["10.0.0.53", "10.0.1.53"]`). Set this when LDAP works in the office but fails on VPN: Node's resolver bypasses Windows NRPT / VPN split-DNS rules, so pointing SRV queries directly at corporate DNS by IP makes discovery deterministic everywhere. Empty = system resolver.
config.aiSharePoint.servicenow.oauthClie: **Machine-scoped.** OAuth client ID for **ServiceNow browser sign-in** — from a one-time Application Registry entry (System OAuth → *Create an OAuth API endpoint for external clients*) with redirect URL `http://localhost:51725/callback`. Users then sign in via their browser SSO session; no passwords or hand-issued tokens. Leave empty to hide the browser option.
Connect a site, chat with @sharepoint, stay in control of Copilot spend, and know exactly what leaves your machine (nothing, unless you export it).

AI features run through your own Copilot entitlement via the VS Code Language Model API — provided by the **GitHub Copilot Chat** extension (marketplace search: “github copilot chat”). No extra keys, nothing routed through third parties.
This step completes automatically when Copilot Chat is installed and you are signed in.
[Install GitHub Copilot Chat](command:aiSharePoint.installCopilotChat)
[Check Copilot Status](command:aiSharePoint.checkCopilotStatus)

Sign in with your Microsoft account (browser or device-code) and connect a site as **managed** or read-only **reference**. Credentials are cached only in your OS keychain.
[Connect a Site](command:aiSharePoint.connectSite)

Ask **@sharepoint** about your sites in the Chat view — try `/site` for a live overview. In agent mode, Copilot can call the read-only tools itself: site overviews, pages, reference sources (Confluence/Jira/LDAP), bookmarks, and usage.
[Open Chat](command:workbench.action.chat.open?%22%40sharepoint%20%2Fhelp%22)

The **Projects** view groups reference sources into a named scope, carries your **goals & instructions** for @sharepoint, and keeps a separate **AI-managed context** the assistant learns over time (you approve each saved note). Activate a project to scope chat and the Reference Sources view to it — everything travels with Export/Import Reference Config.
[Open the Projects View](command:aiSharePoint.projectsView.focus)
[Create a Project](command:aiSharePoint.createProject)
See what the extension asked Copilot

The Copilot Activity view and dashboard show factual, locally measured request counts (today, this month, by model and task). Premium-request consumption against your plan is **not** estimated — your GitHub billing page is the authoritative source.
[Open Activity Dashboard](command:aiSharePoint.showUsage)

Usage counters and redacted error reports are stored **locally only**. When you need support, export an **anonymized bundle** — previewed and leak-scanned before a single byte is written.
[Export Diagnostics Bundle](command:aiSharePoint.exportDiagnostics)
[Privacy & Data Notice](command:aiSharePoint.openPrivacyNotice)
